skill.name: billing-automation
skill.description: Build automated billing systems for recurring payments, invoicing, subscription lifecycle, and dunning management. Use when implementing subscription billing, automating invoicing, or managing recurring payments.
skill.summary: Automated billing system implementation guide for SaaS subscriptions, invoicing, and payment processing
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: All 35 static findings are false positives. The external_commands detections are markdown code block fences (backticks), not shell execution. The network detection is a placeholder URL in example email templates. The blocker detections for weak crypto and system reconnaissance are standard Python code patterns (string assignments, ID generation) used in legitimate billing logic. No prompt injection attempts detected.

high_findings.title: False Positive: external_commands Detection
high_findings.description: Static scanner detected backticks in markdown as shell execution. These are actually markdown code fences (```python ... ```) containing Python code examples for billing automation.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The backtick characters are part of markdown syntax for code blocks, not Ruby/shell command execution. The file contains educational Python code samples for billing implementation.

high_findings.title: False Positive: Hardcoded URL Detection
high_findings.description: Static scanner flagged a URL in example code as potential data exfiltration. This is a placeholder URL in an email template example.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL 'https://app.example.com/billing' is a placeholder in an email template example, not an actual network request to external service.

high_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
high_findings.description: Static scanner flagged string operations as weak crypto. These are invoice description fields, not cryptographic code.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged code contains invoice line item descriptions and table HTML - plain text strings, not cryptographic algorithms.

high_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance
high_findings.description: Static scanner flagged ID generation functions as system reconnaissance. These are standard billing system patterns.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: generate_id() and customer_id assignments are standard billing system code patterns, not reconnaissance or system discovery.

content.value_statement: This skill helps developers implement subscription billing, automate invoicing, and manage payment lifecycles. It provides patterns for recurring payments, dunning management, proration, and tax calculations.
actual_capabilities: Create subscription lifecycle management with trial, active, past_due, and canceled states
Implement automated invoicing with line items, tax calculations, and PDF generation
Build dunning management workflows for failed payment recovery
Calculate prorated charges for plan upgrades, downgrades, and seat changes
Handle multi-region tax calculations including VAT, GST, and sales tax
limitations: Does not integrate with specific payment processors (Stripe, PayPal, etc.)
Does not provide actual payment processing or merchant account setup
Does not generate real invoices for actual customer billing
Requires additional implementation for production payment security

use_cases.title: SaaS subscription management
use_cases.description: Implement recurring billing for SaaS products with monthly, annual, or custom billing cycles

use_cases.title: Automated invoicing system
use_cases.description: Generate and deliver invoices automatically with tax calculations and multiple format outputs

use_cases.title: Payment recovery workflows
use_cases.description: Set up dunning processes to recover failed payments through retries and customer notifications

prompt_templates.title: Basic subscription setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a subscription billing system with monthly and annual plans. Include customer registration, plan selection, and initial billing cycle setup.
prompt_templates.scenario: Setting up a new SaaS billing system from scratch

prompt_templates.title: Invoice generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate invoices for completed subscriptions. Include line items for base plan, usage charges, and applicable tax. Output as PDF.
prompt_templates.scenario: Creating automated invoice generation for recurring bills

prompt_templates.title: Dunning workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Implement a dunning management process. When a payment fails, send customer notifications, retry on schedule, and handle subscription status changes.
prompt_templates.scenario: Handling failed payments and reducing churn

prompt_templates.title: Proration for plan changes
prompt_templates.prompt: Calculate prorated charges when a customer upgrades from basic to pro plan mid-cycle. Include seat additions and billing frequency changes.

output_examples.input: Create a monthly subscription for customer acme_corp on the pro plan
output_examples.output: Subscription created: ID=sub_abc123, Customer=acme_corp, Plan=pro_monthly, Status=trial, Next billing=2024-02-01, Trial ends=2024-01-15

output_examples.input: Generate invoice for subscription sub_abc123 with usage charges
output_examples.output: Invoice INV-2024-001 created: Customer=acme_corp, Line items=[Pro Plan $99.00, API Calls $12.50], Subtotal=$111.50, Tax (CA 7.25%)=$8.08, Total=$119.58

output_examples.input: Process failed payment for customer acme_corp
output_examples.output: Payment failed: Card declined. Dunning initiated: Attempt 1/3, Next retry in 3 days, Email notification sent to billing@acme.com

best_practices: Always test billing logic with sandbox payments before production deployment
Implement idempotency keys for payment requests to prevent duplicate charges
Store complete billing audit trails for compliance and dispute resolution
Use webhooks to synchronize payment status with your database
anti_patterns: Do not charge customers without explicit confirmation or trial consent
Avoid hardcoding prices - use configuration for plan pricing
Do not skip tax calculations, even for digital goods
Never store raw credit card numbers - use payment processor tokens

faq.question: Does this skill connect to Stripe or PayPal?
faq.answer: No, this skill provides implementation patterns and code examples. You will need to integrate with your chosen payment processor separately using their SDKs.

faq.question: Can I use this for one-time payments, not subscriptions?
faq.answer: The skill focuses on recurring billing. For one-time payments, you would adapt the invoice generation portion without the subscription lifecycle management.

faq.question: How do I handle taxes for international customers?
faq.answer: Use the tax calculator pattern with region-specific rates. For VAT/GST, consider integrating with services like Avalara or TaxJar for automated compliance.

faq.question: What happens if a payment fails repeatedly?
faq.answer: The dunning workflow defines retry schedules. After exhausting retries (typically 3-4 attempts over 2-3 weeks), the subscription is canceled or downgraded to a free tier.

faq.question: Can I customize the billing cycles?
faq.answer: Yes, the patterns support weekly, monthly, quarterly, annual, and custom billing intervals. You define the cycle in the subscription configuration.

faq.question: Is this compliant with PCI DSS?
faq.answer: The skill provides billing logic patterns. For PCI compliance, ensure you never handle raw card data - use payment processor tokens and their secure APIs.
